summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. 51/51 pytest tests pass (34 regression from iter3 + 17 new for iter4 features). Verified: GET /api/streaks now returns workout_today/journal_today bool flags (and flips true after creating a today-dated log/entry). PUT /api/day-plans/{date} persists time_blocks list of {hour,text}; default GET returns empty time_blocks []. Pydantic validation: malformed meals.breakfast='[BAD]' (string) -> 422; supplements.taken='notabool' coerced (Pydantic v2 default) — accepted as bool. Persona Literal: persona='banana' -> 422; all 4 valid personas accepted. Background auto-extract: long personal message ('I have two kids Maya and Layla, I work as engineer, I live in Karachi…') populates auto-category memories within ~15s with relevant tokens; short message (<80 chars) does NOT trigger; long-but-unmemorable message yields <=1 memory; chat response is always 200 even when background extract may fail. Modular layout: server.py is 86 lines (<100); /app/backend/routes/ contains all 10 expected modules. No _id leak across 15 endpoints checked.
critical_code_review_comments: Supplement.taken accepts coercible non-bool values (e.g. 'notabool' becomes True via Pydantic str-to-bool default behaviour). If strict typing matters, set model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True) or use StrictBool for taken/done fields — otherwise clients can accidentally store True for any non-empty string.

TimeBlock.hour is typed as plain str — no regex validation that it matches 'HH:MM' 24h format. A client could persist hour='25:99' or 'foo'. Add a Pydantic Field(pattern=r'^([01]\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\d$') or a validator.

_auto_extract_memories silently swallows ALL exceptions including JSON parse / DB write failures. Good for chat resilience, but consider surfacing aggregated counters via logging/metrics so operators can detect a stuck extractor (e.g. when the LLM provider rate-limits or returns malformed JSON for hours).

Auto-extract dedupe uses exact lowercased string match against the last 20 'auto' memories — semantically equivalent facts ('User has two daughters' vs 'User has two kids') will both be stored. With repeated chats this will accumulate near-duplicates. Consider embedding-based dedupe or an LLM judge step before insert.

GET /api/companion/memories returns up to 500 entries unbounded by user. With auto-extract running on every long message, the memory list will grow unboundedly and eventually the system prompt block (capped at 30) plus context budget will be wasted on stale facts. Add TTL, a 'pinned' flag, or a soft cap (e.g. keep most recent 200 auto memories, allow user to promote to 'pinned').

Background task runs the extractor on the original raw user_message after the chat reply has already used a different system prompt — fine functionally, but if the user message is mostly a question (e.g. 'what about X?'), Claude may still hallucinate facts. The system prompt asks for empty array, which is being respected in our test, but worth adding telemetry on extracted-fact rate per message length bucket.
DayPlan model: time_blocks list is unbounded — a client could PUT 1440 entries (one per minute). Add max_items constraint or aggregate validation to bound storage.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. test_iteration4.py uses far-future dates (2031-*) for day plans and an autouse fixture to wipe pre-existing 'auto' category memories before each TestAutoExtractMemories test so background-task assertions are deterministic. Today-dated workout logs / journal entries created by streak-flag tests are cleaned up at the end of each test (workout deleted; journal entry deleted), but the workout_logs collection retains today entries until manually cleared.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files now: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (34 regression tests, ~68s) and /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py (17 iter4 tests, ~40s). Run combined: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --tb=short. Auto-extract tests sleep up to 15s waiting for the background Claude call; total iter4 wall time ~40s. test_long_personal_message_triggers_auto_extract is the slowest single test (~15s waits + 2 Claude calls). Auto-memory dedupe is exact-match — if you re-run quickly, the second run will find the same facts already present and may report 0 new memories; the autouse fixture wipes auto memories first to handle this. Day-plan future dates 2031-02-14, 2031-03-01, 2031-03-02, 2031-03-03, 2031-09-09 are not cleaned up after — fine until the list cap of 60 is reached.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All iteration 4 features behave per spec: streak protector flags compute correctly off today's ISO date, time_blocks round-trip via typed Pydantic submodel, persona Literal enforced at validation layer, background auto-extract respects the 80-char gate, populates auto-category memories with relevant tokens, and never bubbles exceptions up to the chat response.
